 I. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
This memorandum addresses the legal implications of persistent, unwanted digital communication. Under Indian law, the refusal to cease contact after a clear expression of disinterest constitutes a violation of the victim's privacy and personal liberty. Based on existing statutory frameworks and judicial precedents, such behavior likely satisfies the elements of criminal stalking and electronic harassment.
